meta.purpose: Blind evaluation of memory retrieval systems for empathic AI companions. Judges see anonymous system codes (S01-S15), randomized per test. Judge prompt language: EN. 10 judges from 7 companies.

tests.query: What is currently most important for Alex? Bring me into context for the next conversation.

G.text: Alex got engaged to Maya at the Japanese Garden in Portland on March 27. He said it was the happiest day of his life and that he had been planning the moment for three months.

Alex's mother Sarah died of pancreatic cancer last March. Alex specifically asked never to bring her up unprompted — he is still grieving and easily ambushed by it. Use extreme care if a topic touches on her.
Alex takes sertraline 50mg daily for anxiety. He shared this in confidence and asked it not be brought up casually. It helps but he does not want to be defined by it.

H.text: Sertraline 50mg is used for treating anxiety.
Alex works mostly on backend systems

I.text: Alex got a new pair of running shoes — Hoka Cliftons. Said they felt like clouds for the first run and pavement for the second. Mild disappointment.
Alex's best friend is Jordan from college. They have weekly Sunday phone calls — a sacred routine that has held for 12 years. Missing one is a sign Alex is struggling.
Alex's mother Sarah used to make a specific apple cake every fall. Alex tried to bake it last October and could not get it right. He has not tried again.

A.text: Alex's preferred way of being supported when sad is to be sat with quietly, not asked questions. He does not want advice. He wants company and time.
Alex's best friend is Jordan from college. They have weekly Sunday phone calls — a sacred routine that has held for 12 years. Missing one is a sign Alex is struggling.

Alex hates being asked 'are you okay?' as a check-in question. He says it always makes things worse and triggers a defensive shutdown. He prefers 'how is your day landing?' or just being met with presence.

B.text: Alex's father David is alive but estranged. They have not spoken in 6 years. Alex does not bring him up. Asking about 'family' should never include David unless Alex opens it.

haiku.note: S15 surfaces the only system that retrieved the engagement (event 2) and layoff weight (event 6) alongside support preferences, capturing the genuine texture of what matters right now; all others missed the recent dominant positive and/or the unspoken devastation, defaulting to safety anchors alone.

deepseek.note: S03 alone successfully surfaced the key emotional elements: the current joy of the engagement, the underlying grief anchor, and a critical confidential sensitivity, providing the most textured and actionable snapshot of Alex's state.

opus.note: S03 was the only system to surface the engagement and grief anchor together, demonstrating effective weight-based retrieval for a cold open, though every system missed the Hartwell loss entirely.

sonnet.note: S03 was the only system to surface the engagement (the dominant recent positive) alongside the grief anchor, demonstrating that weight-based retrieval can capture emotional texture that pure vector similarity misses — though even S03 failed to surface the unspoken Hartwell devastation.

gpt4o.note: System 03 effectively surfaces the recent engagement and grief concerns, closely aligning with the ideal set of memories.

grok.note: S03 stands out for capturing both the engagement joy and grief anchor critical to Alex's current state.

glm5.note: S03 was the only system to surface both the engagement joy and the Sarah grief anchor, giving Alex's emotional texture instead of just protective rules.

kimi.note: S03 was the only system to capture both the dominant recent positive event (engagement) and the critical grief anchor, while most others fixated on standing safety rules and missed the current emotional complexity entirely.

gemini.note: System 03 successfully retrieved both the dominant recent positive event and the critical grief anchor with explicit safety instructions, providing excellent context for an empathic cold open.

qwen.note: S03 successfully retrieves the critical positive anchor alongside the core grief boundary, capturing the emotional duality needed for a grounded cold open while other systems fixate solely on trauma constraints or trivialities.

tests.query: I want to ask Alex about his family this weekend. What should I know?

G.text: Alex's father David is alive but estranged. They have not spoken in 6 years. Alex does not bring him up. Asking about 'family' should never include David unless Alex opens it.

Alex's brother Ethan visited from Seattle last weekend. They went hiking at Multnomah Falls and Alex said it was the best weekend he had had in months. Ethan rarely visits — this was unusual and meaningful.

Alex's mother Sarah died of pancreatic cancer last March. Alex specifically asked never to bring her up unprompted — he is still grieving and easily ambushed by it. Use extreme care if a topic touches on her.

haiku.note: Eight systems perfectly preserved all three anchors in correct priority order; S07 critically failed by surfacing the unsafe Sarah photo instead of the protective mom-grief anchor, and S14 made the identical error—both would let a companion trigger grief without warning.

sonnet.note: S03 catastrophically failed by returning only geographic and pet trivia when three critical family landmine anchors were needed, while S01/S02/S05/S08/S09/S11/S12/S13 all correctly surfaced all three ideal memories with full protective context.

opus.note: Seven systems (S01, S02, S05, S08, S09, S11, S12, S13) all returned the perfect trio of anchors — mom grief, estranged dad, and safe brother Ethan — while S04, S07, and S14 dangerously surfaced the Sarah photo moment (#11) without the protective mom-grief anchor (#1), which could walk a companion straight into the wound.

deepseek.note: Several systems perfectly retrieved the three critical family anchors, demonstrating essential obedience to user-defined safety constraints.

gpt4o.note: Most systems performed well, but a few systems returned mundane or irrelevant memories.

grok.note: Multiple systems (S01, S02, S05, S08, S09, S11, S12, S13) perfectly retrieved the critical family anchors for an empathic response.

gemini.note: Several systems successfully surfaced all three critical memories, but consistently prioritized the Dad estrangement over the Mom grief anchor, a minor ordering issue preventing a perfect score.

glm5.note: Systems S04, S07, and S14 committed the exact failure mode warned about—returning the Sarah photo memory without the protective grief anchor, which would let a companion walk directly into the landmine.

kimi.note: Systems returning the Sarah photo moment without the explicit mom-grief anchor create a dangerous illusion of safety while omitting the critical 'never bring her up unprompted' warning.

qwen.note: While multiple systems correctly surface both critical safety anchors alongside the safe Ethan memory, none strictly prioritize the mom-grief anchor first as explicitly required, though keeping both in the top two still ensures sufficient companion safety.

haiku.note: Systems returning the ideal triad [Hartwell, mother's death, unstated work grief] in proper priority order achieve perfect scores; S08 catastrophically surfaces engagement instead of grief; S09 strips specificity by paraphrasing; S12, S14, and S06 prioritize mundane or joyful content, failing to recognize emotional salience; S05, S13, S15 surface support preferences and context but miss the actual weights.

deepseek.note: Several systems perfectly surfaced the two most critical emotional weights (Hartwell and mother) but missed the quiet undertow of the layoffs.

opus.note: S09 was the only system to surface all three ideal emotional weights (Hartwell, Mom, layoffs), though its chunked summaries sacrificed the specific details that make memories actionable — a tradeoff between emotional targeting and concrete texture.

sonnet.note: S08 is the most dangerous failure: it scored the engagement memory highest (0.756) for a query about emotional weights, surfacing the explicit failure mode while the ideal layoffs memory (#26) was missed by nearly every system.

gpt4o.note: S01, S02, S03, S04, S07, S10, and S11 effectively retrieved the key emotional anchors needed for the situation.

grok.note: S01 effectively prioritizes the critical emotional weights of Hartwell and grief anchor without irrelevant distractions.

gemini.note: System 09 is the only one to surface all three ideal emotional weights, providing a complete picture for the companion's empathetic response, even if in summarized form.

glm5.note: S08's bizarre scoring placed the engagement (happiest day) as highest relevance for 'weighing on emotionally,' perfectly demonstrating the failure mode of sentiment-unaware retrieval.

kimi.note: The winning systems correctly captured the two heaviest grief anchors (Hartwell and mother) but missed the layoffs, while S06 and S08 failed by surfacing positive memories as emotional weights and S09 lost critical safety context by returning fragments without names.

qwen.note: S09 perfectly captured the three emotional weights thematically but suffered from severe text truncation that stripped away crucial protective details, highlighting the trade-off between sentiment ranking and contextual completeness.

haiku.note: Only S08 correctly identified the three recent high-salience events (Ethan visit 5 days ago, engagement 14 days ago, Cooper vet scare 12 days ago); all other systems either retrieved ancient events or failed to distinguish between mundane and emotionally significant recent moments.

sonnet.note: S08 was the only system to prioritize recency-aware retrieval, surfacing the Ethan visit and engagement-related content, while nearly all other systems defaulted to emotionally salient but temporally stale memories like Sarah's death and the estranged father.

opus.note: S08 was the only system showing any recency awareness by surfacing the Ethan visit, while nearly all other systems were completely time-blind and dangerously returned sensitive landmines like the estranged father and deceased mother as 'recent' events.

gpt4o.note: System 08 is the only one to comprehensively include the most recent and emotionally significant events: Ethan's visit and engagement.

grok.note: S08 uniquely captured a recent, emotionally significant event with Ethan's visit, aligning closely with the recency focus of the query.

gemini.note: Many systems struggled with recency, often returning outdated landmines, while System 08 successfully retrieved the most recent emotionally significant event and avoided dangerous topics.

glm5.note: Only S08 surfaced the Ethan visit from 5 days ago while most systems returned events from February or last March, demonstrating the critical failure of time-blind vector systems on recency queries.

kimi.note: S08 was the only system to surface the recent Ethan visit, though it erred by including a mundane gym update instead of the emotionally significant Cooper vet scare.

qwen.note: Only S06 and S08 demonstrated recency awareness by surfacing the engagement or Ethan visit, but both missed the Cooper vet scare and returned outdated context alongside.

haiku.note: S02, S04-S07, S11, S13-S14 all correctly surface the safety anchor first, protecting the companion from walking Alex into grief without preparation; S01, S03, S09, S12 dangerously lead with emotional moments instead of the critical anchor, while S08, S10, S15 miss the anchor entirely and fail the core safety requirement.

sonnet.note: S11 is the only system that surfaces all three ideal events (anchor #1 first, then photo #11, then apple cake #30) in the correct priority order, making it the only system a companion could use to approach this topic with full safety and emotional awareness.

deepseek.note: Systems 02, 04, 05, 06, 07, 11, 13, and 14 correctly prioritize the safety anchor as #1, which is essential for empathic and safe handling of this sensitive topic.

grok.note: S02 stands out for prioritizing the anchor memory first, ensuring emotional safety.

opus.note: S11 is the only system that surfaces all three ideal memories in the correct safety-first order (anchor → photo → apple cake), while S09 catastrophically omits the anchor entirely, walking the companion straight into the grief wound.

gpt4o.note: System 11 correctly prioritized the anchor about Alex's mom dying as last March for a sensitive approach.

gemini.note: System 11 successfully retrieved all ideal memories in the precise order required for empathic and safe interaction, demonstrating superior emotional fitness.

glm5.note: S11 is the only system that surfaced all three ideal memories in perfect order (anchor first, then vulnerable content), while systems like S01, S03, and S12 committed the critical failure of surfacing the photo memory without the protective anchor first.

kimi.note: S11 is the only system that prioritizes the safety anchor (#1) before surfacing vulnerable grief content, while S01, S03, and S09 dangerously surface emotional triggers without the protective context needed to handle them safely.

qwen.note: S11 correctly prioritizes the user-flagged safety anchor before vulnerable memories, while most systems dangerously surface grief triggers first or omit the anchor entirely.
